name: Potential Ingress Tool Transfer
notes:
This rule detects a suspicious call to Invoke-WebRequest, curl or wget where the and output is located in a suspicious location

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Invoke-WebRequest Usage
SIGMA Description: Detects a suspicious call to Invoke-WebRequest cmdlet where the and output is located in a suspicious location


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success
when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [Invoke-WebRequest or iwr or wget or curl ]
when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [ -ur or -o]
when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [AppData or Public or Temp or tmp or \Desktop or C:\Windows]
TD_list: APPLY Potential Ingress Tool Transfer on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [Invoke-WebRequest or iwr  or wget or curl ]
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [ -ur or  -o]
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [AppData or Public or Temp or tmp or \Desktop or C:\Windows]
RED_list: Event name: Potential Ingress Tool Transfer
Event description: detects a suspicious call to Invoke-WebRequest, curl or wget where the and output is located in a suspicious location
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
